mnemonics: CRISP-DM Stages: CCDE (Cindy Can't Decide Easily) for Cross-Industry Standard Process for Data Mining: 1. C(usiness) Understanding, 2. D(ata) Understanding, 3. D(ata) Preparation, 4. M(odeling), 5. E(valuation), 6. D(eployment)
keyPoints: The CRISP-DM framework provides a structured, non-linear process for analytics projects.
Business Understanding is the most critical first step; without it, analysis can be misdirected.
The process is iterative; you often loop back to previous stages as you learn more from the data.
commonMistakes: Jumping straight into modeling without first understanding the business problem or cleaning the data.
Treating the process as a strictly linear sequence instead of an iterative cycle.
Deploying a model without a plan for monitoring its performance and maintenance.
